title: Agent-Memory-Paper-List: The paper list of 'Memory in the Age of AI Agents: A Survey'
snippet: Curated paper list for the survey 'Memory in the Age of AI Agents'. Covers memory mechanisms for LLM-based agents with unified taxonomy across form, function, and dynamics.

metadata.llm_reason: Directly relevant curated paper list for the most comprehensive memory survey, excellent entry point for researchers
metadata.author: Shichun-Liu

title: From Storage to Experience: A Survey on the Evolution of LLM Agent Memory Mechanisms [v2]
snippet: Proposes evolutionary framework: Storage (trajectory preservation) -> Reflection (trajectory refinement) -> Experience (trajectory abstraction). Three-stage evolution of agent memory.

metadata.llm_reason: Novel evolutionary framework for understanding memory architecture progression, directly addresses the survey topic

title: A Survey on the Memory Mechanism of Large Language Model-based Agents
snippet: ACM TOIS survey covering memory mechanisms. First comprehensive taxonomy of memory designs in LLM agents with formal classification across temporal scope, representational substrate, and control policy.

metadata.llm_reason: Major venue (ACM TOIS) survey providing formal taxonomy of memory mechanisms - authoritative reference

title: A-Mem: Agentic Memory for LLM Agents
snippet: Applies Zettelkasten principles to create interconnected knowledge networks through dynamic indexing and linking. NeurIPS 2025 paper. Enables agents to dynamically organize memories.

metadata.llm_reason: Novel Zettelkasten-inspired approach to agent memory at top venue (NeurIPS 2025), represents new design pattern
metadata.author: Wujiang Xu et al.

title: Continuum Memory Architectures for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
snippet: Defines CMA as systems maintaining and updating internal state through persistent storage, selective retention, associative routing, temporal chaining, and consolidation into higher-order abstractions.

metadata.llm_reason: Introduces formal definition of Continuum Memory Architecture - a new theoretical framework for long-horizon agents

title: Memory for Autonomous LLM Agents: Mechanisms, Evaluation, and Emerging Frontiers
snippet: March 2026 survey formalizing agent memory as write-manage-read loop. Three-dimensional taxonomy: temporal scope, representational substrate, control policy. Five mechanism families.

metadata.llm_reason: Most recent comprehensive survey (March 2026) with formal write-manage-read loop framework and five mechanism families

title: Agentic Memory: Learning Unified Long-Term and Short-Term Memory Management for Large Language Model Agents
snippet: AgeMem integrates long-term and short-term memory management into agent policy, exposing memory operations as tool-based actions. Agents autonomously decide what/when to store, retrieve, update, summarize, or discard.

metadata.llm_reason: Key paper on unified memory management through tool-based actions - bridges theoretical architecture with practical agent implementation

title: The 6 Best AI Agent Memory Frameworks You Should Try in 2026
snippet: Practical overview comparing top memory frameworks for AI agents in 2026 including Mem0, Letta, Zep, and others with implementation guidance.

metadata.llm_reason: Practical comparison of top frameworks with implementation guidance, useful for practitioners

title: ICLR 2026 Workshop on Memory for LLM-Based Agentic Systems (MemAgents)
snippet: Workshop bridging RL, memory research, LLMs, agentic systems, and neuroscience. Topics: episodic/semantic/working memory, parametric knowledge, knowledge graphs, vector DBs, retrieval pipelines. April 26-27, 2026.
query: ICLR 2026 workshop MemAgents memory LLM agentic systems

metadata.llm_reason: Major academic workshop signal showing memory is a first-class research topic at top venue, defines the field's research agenda

title: SimpleMem: Efficient Lifelong Memory for LLM Agents
snippet: Semantic lossless compression for long-term memory. Three-stage pipeline: instantly integrates related context into unified abstract representations to eliminate redundancy.
query: persistent memory LLM agent framework github 2025 2026

metadata.llm_reason: Novel compression-based approach to lifelong memory, addresses key scalability challenge

title: MemOS: AI memory OS for LLM and Agent systems
snippet: Memory operating system enabling persistent skill memory for cross-task skill reuse and evolution. v2.0 Stardust (Dec 2025) with knowledge base, memory feedback, multi-modal memory, tool memory.
query: persistent memory LLM agent framework github 2025 2026

metadata.llm_reason: Memory-as-OS paradigm with skill persistence and cross-task reuse - important architectural pattern

title: Memori: SQL Native Memory Layer for LLMs, AI Agents and Multi-Agent Systems
snippet: SQL-native memory layer achieving 81.95% on LoCoMo. LLM, datastore, and framework agnostic. Seamless integration into existing architectures.
query: persistent memory LLM agent framework github 2025 2026

metadata.llm_reason: SQL-native approach is architecturally distinct from vector-DB approaches, with strong benchmark results

title: ReMe: Memory Management Kit for Agents
snippet: Tackles limited context window and stateless sessions. Auto-compacts old conversations, persistently stores important information, automatically recalls relevant context in future interactions.
query: persistent memory LLM agent framework github 2025 2026

metadata.llm_reason: Practical memory management kit solving core agent statefulness challenges

title: Awesome-AI-Memory: Curated knowledge base on AI memory for LLMs and agents
snippet: Covers long-term memory, reasoning, retrieval, memory-native system design. Systematic collection of research, frameworks, benchmarks, and applications.

snippet: Tsinghua C3I lab's curated collection of papers about memory for language agents.

title: Letta: Platform for building stateful agents with advanced memory
snippet: AI with advanced memory that can learn and self-improve over time. Formerly MemGPT. OS-inspired virtual context management with tiered memory and paging.

metadata.llm_reason: Core project in the field - the open-source reference implementation for OS-inspired memory management
metadata.author: Letta AI

title: Letta Code: A Memory-First Coding Agent
snippet: #1 model-agnostic open source agent on Terminal-Bench. Git-backed memory, skills, subagents, and deployment across every model provider. Remote environments March 2026.

metadata.llm_reason: Shows practical application of memory architecture to coding agents - validates memory-first approach in production

title: Stateful AI Agents: A Deep Dive into Letta (MemGPT) Memory Models
snippet: Feb 2026 deep dive into Letta's memory model architecture including core block, conversation history, recall memory, and archival memory tiers.

metadata.llm_reason: Detailed architectural analysis of Letta's memory tiers - useful for understanding implementation details

title: Mem0 - The Memory Layer for your AI Apps
snippet: Universal memory layer. $24M Series A. 41K+ GitHub stars, 13M+ PyPI downloads. Processes 186M+ API calls/quarter. 80K+ developers. AWS Agent SDK exclusive memory provider.

metadata.llm_reason: Market-leading commercial memory platform with strong adoption metrics, essential for commercial landscape coverage
metadata.author: Mem0 AI

title: Mem0 raises $24M from YC, Peak XV and Basis Set
snippet: $24M total funding ($3.9M seed + $20M Series A). Q1 2025: 35M API calls. Q3: 186M API calls (30% MoM growth). 26% higher accuracy than OpenAI's memory on LOCOMO.

metadata.llm_reason: Key funding and growth data for the leading commercial memory startup, validates market demand

title: Mem0: Building Production-Ready AI Agents with Scalable Long-Term Memory
snippet: Technical paper on Mem0's architecture. Achieves 67.13% LLM-as-Judge on LOCOMO with p95 search latency 0.200s using ~1,764 tokens vs 26,031 for full-context.

metadata.llm_reason: Technical details on production memory architecture with concrete efficiency metrics (15x token reduction)

title: AI Agent Memory Systems in 2026: Mem0, Zep, Hindsight, Memvid and Everything In Between — Compared
snippet: Comprehensive comparison of all major memory systems in 2026 including architecture differences, benchmark results, and use-case recommendations.

metadata.llm_reason: Comprehensive comparison article covering the full landscape with benchmarks - excellent for synthesis

title: LongMemEval: Benchmarking Chat Assistants on Long-Term Interactive Memory (ICLR 2025)
snippet: 500 manually created questions testing 5 core memory abilities. Simulates 115K-token and up to 1.5M-token settings. ICLR 2025 paper. 30%-60% performance drop in long-context LLMs.

metadata.llm_reason: Premier benchmark for memory evaluation at top venue (ICLR 2025), reveals critical performance gaps
metadata.author: xiaowu0162

title: LoCoMo: Evaluating Very Long-Term Conversational Memory of LLM Agents
snippet: Long-term conversational memory benchmark using multi-session conversation logs with rich temporal, personal, and event-driven grounding. Primary testbed for memory system evaluation.

metadata.llm_reason: Foundational benchmark from Snap Research, used as primary evaluation by Mem0, Hindsight, and others
metadata.author: Snap Research

title: Benchmarking and Enhancing Long-Term Memory in LLMs
snippet: Systematic benchmarking framework for long-term memory capabilities of LLMs with enhancement strategies.

metadata.llm_reason: Dedicated benchmarking paper for long-term memory - fills evaluation gap

title: MemoryCD: Benchmarking Long-Context User Memory of LLM Agents for Lifelong Cross-Domain Personalization
snippet: Multi-faceted evaluation pipeline: 14 SOTA LLM base models, 6 memory method baselines, 4 personalization tasks, 12 diverse domains. March 2026.

metadata.llm_reason: Latest benchmark (March 2026) covering cross-domain personalization - expands evaluation beyond conversation to real applications

title: Multi-Agent Memory from a Computer Architecture Perspective: Visions and Challenges Ahead
snippet: Position paper framing multi-agent memory as computer architecture problem. Three-layer hierarchy (I/O, cache, memory). Two protocol gaps: cache sharing and structured access control.

metadata.llm_reason: Novel framing of multi-agent memory as computer architecture problem - introduces formal hierarchy model

title: Collaborative Memory: Multi-User Memory Sharing in LLM Agents with Dynamic Access Control
snippet: Framework for multi-user, multi-agent environments with asymmetric, time-evolving access controls. Two memory tiers: private and shared with selective fragments.

metadata.llm_reason: Addresses practical multi-user memory sharing with access control - key for enterprise deployment

title: Intrinsic Memory Agents: Heterogeneous Multi-Agent LLM Systems through Structured Contextual Memory
snippet: Agent-specific memories evolving intrinsically with agent outputs. Addresses context window limitations in multi-agent systems.

metadata.llm_reason: Intrinsic memory approach for heterogeneous multi-agent systems - distinct from shared-memory approaches

title: Your 1M+ Context Window LLM Is Less Powerful Than You Think
snippet: Lost-in-the-middle phenomenon: early/late context 85-95% accuracy, middle sections 76-82%. Large context windows are not a silver bullet for memory.
query: context window vs external memory LLM debate 2025

metadata.llm_reason: Quantifies the lost-in-the-middle problem with specific accuracy numbers, supports case for external memory

title: Cognitive Workspace: Active Memory Management for LLMs
snippet: Novel paradigm transcending traditional RAG by emulating human cognitive mechanisms of external memory use. Selective approach to context window usage.
query: context window vs external memory LLM debate 2025

metadata.llm_reason: Cognitive Workspace is a new paradigm bridging context-window and external-memory approaches

title: Zep: A Temporal Knowledge Graph Architecture for Agent Memory
snippet: Graphiti temporal knowledge graph engine. Outperforms MemGPT on DMR benchmark (94.8% vs 93.4%). 18.5% accuracy improvement with 90% latency reduction vs baselines.
query: Zep AI memory knowledge graph agent 2025 2026

metadata.llm_reason: Temporal knowledge graph approach with strong benchmark results - represents graph-based memory architecture
metadata.author: Preston Rasmussen / Zep

title: Graphiti: Build Real-Time Knowledge Graphs for AI Agents
snippet: Open-source temporal knowledge graph engine from Zep. Autonomously builds context graphs from unstructured/structured data with temporal history and validity windows.

metadata.llm_reason: Open-source implementation of temporal knowledge graph for agent memory - practical building block
metadata.author: Zep/Graphiti

title: NAMMs: Neural Attention Memory Models (ICLR 2025)
snippet: Memory evolution framework optimizing transformer models without altering architectures. Trains on pre-trained transformers for performance and efficiency gains on long-context tasks.

metadata.llm_reason: ICLR 2025 paper on memory optimization for transformers - relevant to foundational architecture dimension

title: MELODI: Memory with Low Dimension (ICLR 2026)
snippet: Efficient memory architecture for long contexts on short windows. Integrates short-term (recurrent, multi-layer) and long-term (incremental, single-layer) memory. 8x memory reduction vs Memorizing Transformer.

metadata.llm_reason: ICLR 2026 paper with concrete efficiency gains (8x reduction) - advances the architectural frontier

title: Larimar: Large Language Models with Episodic Memory Control (ICML 2024)
snippet: Brain-inspired architecture from IBM + Princeton. Distributed episodic memory controller inspired by hippocampal complementary learning systems. One-shot knowledge updates without retraining. 4-10x speedup.

metadata.llm_reason: Neuroscience-inspired approach at top venue (ICML 2024), unique complementary learning systems angle
metadata.author: IBM Research + Princeton

title: MemoryBank: Enhancing Large Language Models with Long-Term Memory (AAAI 2024)
snippet: External memory bank with writer/retriever/reader modules. Ebbinghaus forgetting curve for memory intensity updates (R=e^(-t/S)). SiliconFriend chatbot application.

title: MAGMA: A Multi-Graph based Agentic Memory Architecture for AI Agents

title: Mnemosyne: Cognitive Memory OS for AI Agents — zero LLM calls
